title: epipactis recurva
intro: Epipactis recurva, also known as the bent helleborine, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. This perennial plant is native to Europe and Asia, and it has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Epipactis recurva include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Epipactis recurva can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its health benefits.
